description: Side-by-side visual comparison of HTML source slides vs converted PPTX output. Use when checking conversion quality, doing a visual diff, reviewing fidelity, or verifying the PPTX matches the original. Also use proactively after any conversion — "does it look right?", "how close is it?", "check fidelity", "visual diff".

results.query: does the PPTX look close to the original HTML? Show me a side-by-side comparison so I can spot differences
compare my HTML slides in slides/ with the converted deck.pptx — I want to check conversion fidelity
generate comparison images for slides 1, 5, and 8 so I can see where the PPTX diverges from the HTML
how close is the PowerPoint to the original? I want paired screenshots — HTML on one side, PPTX on the other
do a visual diff between the HTML source and the generated PowerPoint. Focus on slides 3 and 7 where I noticed issues
after the conversion, check if the PPTX matches the HTML. I want to catch any visual regressions
is the output faithful to the source? Compare and show me where it drifts
validate my deck for overflow and quality issues — check bounds and text overflow
check my deck for overflow and give me a per-slide quality score
render deck.pptx to PNG images for review — I just want thumbnails, not a comparison
create a contact sheet montage of all slides
convert these HTML slides to PowerPoint — just the conversion step
run the full end-to-end pipeline including standardize, convert, validate, render
the colors are wrong in the PPTX — alpha blending issue
take a Chrome headless screenshot of slide-01.html
create a presentation outline about our product roadmap
normalize these AI-generated slides before conversion
